searchDocs.title: Method #1 - Roblox‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Download straight from the roblox marketplace this will only contain the module itself.

searchDocs.title: Method #2 - Github‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Download straight from the releases subdirectory of this repository

searchDocs.title: Download options‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Install the SecureClasses.zip file for just the runtime moduleInstall the SecureClassesWithTests.zip file for runtime and test scripts

searchDocs.title: Method #3 - Git Clone‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Clone this repository straight into your project with Git

searchDocs.title: TransferMetatable‚Äč
searchDocs.content: ReadOnly </> interface TransferMetatable { Table:¬†T-- Front table template Metatable:¬†MT-- Metatable template IsMetatable:¬†boolean } Middle man object with all its data stored inside of its metatable

searchDocs.title: WriteMetamethods‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> TransferTable:WriteMetamethods( self:¬†TransferMetatable, Metamethods:¬†MetaMethods, Flag:¬†string-- string assigned to __metatable ) ‚Üí¬†ProtectedMetatable Compiles the protected metatable with its protected metamethods

searchDocs.title: Wrapped Metatable‚Äč
searchDocs.content: 1. Creating Transfer class‚Äč  --!strict -- Metatable test local SecureClasses = require("../") -- Front table local T = { ["Test1"] = true } -- Metatable local MT = { ["Test2"] = false, UpdateTest1 = function(self:any, NewValue:any) rawset(self, "Test2", NewValue) end } -- Simple optional type just to help out the type solver type tabletype = {["Test1"]:boolean} & {["Test2"]:boolean} -- Creates a transfer table object local TransferTable = SecureClasses.NewMetatable(T, MT)

searchDocs.title: 2. Assign metamethods‚Äč
searchDocs.content: --!strict -- Having strict enabled will allow the type solver to lint your metamethod table more accuratly local Metamethods:SecureClasses.MetaMethods<tabletype> = { -- Uranary metamethod __unm = function(s) s.Test1 = not s.Test1 s.Test2 = not s.Test2 return s end, -- Concatenation metamethod __concat = function<V>(s, v:V) for i:any, k:any in v::any do (s::any)[i] = k end return s :: any end, -- Destroy metamethod __destroy = function(s) warn("Destroying class") end, } -- Creates a WrappedMetatable class that function the same as a regular metatable -- Table is Read-Only local MetaTable = TransferTable:WriteMetamethods(Metamethods, "Protected Metatable")

searchDocs.title: 3. Basic indexing and writing‚Äč
searchDocs.content: --!strict -- Having strict enabled will allow the type solver to lint your metamethod table more accuratly -- Ok print(MetaTable.Test1) print(MetaTable.Test2) -- Not ok print(MetaTable.Test3) -- Will lock table and metatable with flag -- table can be overwritten with __tostring metamethod print(MetaTable, getmetatable(MetaTable)) -- Not ok will error --MetaTable.Test2 = nil -- Metamethod to update private varible MetaTable:UpdateTest1(20) -- prints new value print(MetaTable.Test2)

searchDocs.title: 4. Destroy‚Äč
searchDocs.content: --!strict -- Having strict enabled will allow the type solver to lint your metamethod table more accuratly -- Destroy with nuke MetaTable:Destroy(true) -- Not Ok print(MetaTable.Test1) print(MetaTable.Test2) -- Empty print(MetaTable, getmetatable(MetaTable))

searchDocs.title: ProtectedMetatable‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type ProtectedMetatable = (T¬†&¬†MT¬†&¬†MM) Table, metatable, and metamethods all linked together, metamethods are private INFO The Secured Metatable class is a ready only wrapper that enacts a read only function apart of the __newindex metamethod, a sandboxed __index table, and the __metatable flag being enacted and is only destroyable by the :Destroy function.

searchDocs.title: MetaMethods‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> interface MetaMethods { __index:¬†(( self:¬†T, index:¬†(keyof(T)¬†|¬†any) )¬†‚Üí¬†()¬†|¬†{[any]:¬†any})?,-- self[keyof(T)] Indexes the table attached (Not the actual Metatable) __newindex:¬†(( self:¬†T, index:¬†any, value:¬†any )¬†‚Üí¬†()¬†|¬†{[any]:¬†any})?,-- self[keyof(T)] = V Adds new value (locked for read only) __call:¬†(( self:¬†T, ...any )¬†‚Üí¬†any)?-- self() function call __concat:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†V )¬†‚Üí¬†T¬†&¬†V)?,-- self..value Concatenation (combine) __unm:¬†((self:¬†T)¬†‚Üí¬†T)?,-- -self Unary (inverse) __iter:¬†(typeof(next))?,-- Generalized iteration (Type of next as default) __add:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†number )¬†‚Üí¬†number)?,-- self+value Addition (Adds a number) __sub:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†number )¬†‚Üí¬†number)?,-- self-value Subtraction (Subtracts a number) __mul:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†number )¬†‚Üí¬†number)?,-- self*value Multiplication (Multiples a number) __div:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†number )¬†‚Üí¬†number)?,-- self/value Division (Divides a number) __idiv:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†number )¬†‚Üí¬†number)?,-- self//value Floor division (Floor divides a number) __mod:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†number )¬†‚Üí¬†number)?,-- self%value Modulus (Modulo's by a number) __pow:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†number )¬†‚Üí¬†number)?,-- self^value Exponentiation (Exponentiates a number) __tostring:¬†((self:¬†T)¬†‚Üí¬†string)?,-- tostring(self) -> printable string () __len:¬†((self:¬†T)¬†‚Üí¬†number)?,-- #self Array length (length) __eq:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†any )¬†‚Üí¬†boolean)?,-- self==value Equality operator (compares a value to be equal) __lt:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†any )¬†‚Üí¬†boolean)?,-- self<value Less than operator (compares a value to be less than) __le:¬†(( self:¬†T, value:¬†any )¬†‚Üí¬†boolean)?,-- self<=value Less than or equal operator (compares a value to be less than or equal) __mode:¬†("K"¬†|¬†"V"¬†|¬†"KV")?,-- Sets the garbage collection rule of the table __metatable:¬†string-- Locks metatable __destroy:¬†(( self:¬†T, OptionalBooleanArgument:boolean )¬†‚Üí¬†())?-- custom function to be assigned when 'Destroy is called' }  Forbidden Metamethods: __index, __newindex, and __metatable are custom and constant and cannot be written or read from. Comparisons __eq, __lt, __le Requires two values with the same metamethod function and basic type (table/userdata/etc.); does not work with a table and another random table, or with a userdata and a table. New Metamethod __destroy can be assigned to be ran before the auto cleanup of :Destroy().

searchDocs.title: NewMetatable‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SecuredMetatable.NewMetatable( Table:¬†T,-- The front table template and inital values Metatable:¬†MT-- The metatable template and inital values ) ‚Üí¬†TransferTable-- A read-only wrapped metatable Creates a new TransferMetatable object that will turn into a protected metatable

searchDocs.title: NewUserdata‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SecuredMetatable.NewUserdata( Table:¬†T,-- The front table template and inital values Metatable:¬†MT-- The metatable template and inital values ) ‚Üí¬†TransferTable-- A read-only wrapped Userdata Creates a new TransferMetatable object that will turn into a protected metatable Read-Only Userdata's are read only by default even without metamethods and cannot be destroyed apart of the :Destroy() funtion. Calling it will result in a error Constant Userdata's are read only by default even without metamethods making them perfect for read only data.

searchDocs.title: Destroy‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SecuredMetatable:Destroy( self:¬†SecuredMetatable, Nuke:¬†boolean-- Complete destroy ) ‚Üí¬†nil The only way to destroy the metatable when flags are assigned Auto clean-up Destroy contains a auto destroy function that can correctly clean up all basic ROBLOX objects and instances. Nuke is used when you want to destroy instances left false it will only delete the reference to the instance. __destroy The custom metamethod __destroy is a metamethod you can assign that will run before the auto destroy function so you can clean up custom complex objects. Note this DOES NOT prevent the auto destroy function from running only it runs __destroy before SmartClean Userdata's Userdata is read only by default and CANNOT be destroyed running :Destroy on a userdata will result in an error
